message.thinking:
We need to answer three questions with JSON array of 3 objects. Each object has keys: question, feedback, complies. The "question" field should be one word: Chapters/Description/Structure depending on which question. So first object question: "Chapters". Second: "Description". Third: "Structure".

We need to analyze the text: It's about exploring steganography in PE files, describing methods: Slack Space, string replacement using Hydan and steg86, dll injections. Practical part: trying to hide info in PE files using these methods, analyze effectiveness and impact.

Questions:

1. Chapters: Does text list the main sections? It doesn't list sections, just gives overview. So no. Feedback: No section headings. Provide suggestions. Complies false.

2. Description: Does text briefly describe contents of each section? Since it doesn't list sections, can't. So no. Complies false.

3. Structure: Can general structure be easily understood? The text outlines main topic and practical part, but not structure. So probably not. Complies false.

But maybe we can say partial compliance: It indicates there is analysis part and practical part, but not full structure. The question: "Can it be easily understood what is the general structure of the thesis just from this text?" It's somewhat implied: analysis of methods, practical application. But not clear. I'd say not fully. So false.

Provide feedback for each accordingly. Ensure JSON array.
